problem_text_cn:
4-27 如图4-25所示，在光滑的水平面上有一个固定的光滑大圆环，一个小的发射装置P紧贴在圆环的内侧，开始时处于静止状态，且头部朝右，尾部朝左。P内存有许多微小的光滑珠子，其总质量与空的发射装置质量相同。某时刻起，P从其尾部不断向后发射小珠子，因珠子微小，发射可以认为是连续进行的。设珠子射出时相对P的速率为常量，单位时间发射的珠子质量也是常量，再设当P的头部与前方运动过来的第一个珠子相遇时，P刚好将其中的珠子全部发射完毕。
(1) 试求P在发射珠子的全过程中，P相对圆环转过的总角度$\theta$（精确到$1^{\circ}$）；
(2) 若P的头部遇到前方运动过来的珠子时，能将珠子吞入其中且不再发射，试确定P相对圆环的最终运动速率$v_{p}$。
problem_text_en:
4-27 As shown in Figure 4-25, on a smooth horizontal plane there is a fixed smooth large ring. A small launching device P is attached to the inner side of the ring; initially it is at rest, with its head pointing right and its tail pointing left. Inside P there are many tiny smooth balls whose total mass equals the mass of the empty device. From a certain instant, P continuously launches balls from its tail; because the balls are tiny, the emission can be considered continuous. Assume that the speed of a ball relative to P at the moment of emission is constant, and the mass emitted per unit time is also constant. Moreover, when the head of P meets the first ball emitted forward, P has just finished launching all the balls.
(1) Find the total angle $\theta$ (to the nearest degree) through which P rotates relative to the ring during the whole process of launching balls.
(2) If the head of P, upon meeting a ball coming from the front, can swallow the ball and not launch it again, determine the final speed $v_{p}$ of P relative to the ring.
problem_solution_cn:
解 (1) 将空的发射装置质量和全部珠子质量记为$m$，珠子相对于P的发射速率记为常量$u$，单位时间内射出的珠子质量记为$\mu$（也是常量）。再将开始发射时刻记为$t=0$，全部发射完时刻记为$t=T$，应有
$$ m = \mu T. $$
某个时刻$t$，P和内含剩余珠子的总质量记为$M$，沿圆环内侧圆运动速率记为$v$，相对环心角动量便是
$$ L = R M v, $$
其中$R$为环半径。经$dt$时间，发射了质量为$-dM$的珠子，这些珠子相对环的反向速率为$u-v$，P的剩余质量为$M+dM$，正向速率增为$v+dv$，两者角动量之和为
$$ \begin{aligned} L^{\prime} &= R\left[(M+dM)(v+dv) - (-dM)(u-v)\right] \\ &= R(Mv + M dv + u dM). \end{aligned} $$
因角动量守恒，有$L^{\prime}=L$，由此得
$$ M dv + u dM = 0. $$
积分，利用$t=0$时$v=0$，$M=2m$，得
$$ v = u \ln\frac{2m}{M}. $$
当$M=m$（即珠子全部发射完）时，
$$ v_p = u \ln 2. $$
从$t=0$到$t=T$，P通过的总路程为
$$ l = \int_0^T v \, dt = u \int_0^T \ln\frac{2m}{M} \, dt. $$
因$\frac{dM}{dt} = -\mu$，$M = 2m - \mu t$，代入积分可得
$$ l = uT \ln\frac{e}{2}. $$
在$t=0$时刻发射的第一个珠子，沿反方向走过的路程为
$$ l_0 = uT. $$
设$l$和$l_0$对应的圆心角分别为$\theta$和$\theta_0$，据题设，当P头部遇到第一个珠子时，有
$$ \theta + \theta_0 = 2\pi, $$
且
$$ \frac{\theta}{\theta_0} = \frac{l}{l_0} = \ln\frac{e}{2}. $$
消去$\theta_0$，解得
$$ \theta = \frac{\ln\frac{e}{2}}{1+\ln\frac{e}{2}} \cdot 2\pi \approx 85^{\circ}. $$
(2) 系统角动量守恒，初始角动量为零，任何时刻角动量都为零。P刚发射完珠子的系统状态如图4-26所示，此时最后发射出的珠子相对圆环逆时针方向速度$u - v_p = u\ln\frac{e}{2} > 0$。而后P将陆续吞入前方的珠子并减速。如果P在尚未吞完全部珠子前，便与剩余的珠子一起顺时针方向运动，那么系统的角动量必不为零，这将与初始值为零矛盾。因此P必能吞入全部珠子，最后速率为
$$ v_p = 0. $$
problem_solution_en:
Solution (1) Denote the mass of the empty launcher and the total mass of the balls as $m$, the constant emission speed of a ball relative to P as $u$, and the constant mass emitted per unit time as $\mu$. Let the emission start at $t=0$ and finish at $t=T$; then
$$ m = \mu T. $$
At time $t$, let the total mass of P plus the remaining balls be $M$, and its speed along the inner side of the ring be $v$. The angular momentum about the centre of the ring is
$$ L = R M v, $$
where $R$ is the radius of the ring. During an infinitesimal time $dt$, a mass $-dM$ of balls is emitted; these balls have a speed $u-v$ relative to the ring in the opposite direction. The remaining mass of P becomes $M+dM$ and its forward speed increases to $v+dv$. The sum of angular momenta is
$$ \begin{aligned} L^{\prime} &= R\left[(M+dM)(v+dv) - (-dM)(u-v)\right] \\ &= R(Mv + M dv + u dM). \end{aligned} $$
Angular momentum conservation $L^{\prime}=L$ gives
$$ M dv + u dM = 0. $$
Integrating, and using $v=0$, $M=2m$ at $t=0$, we obtain
$$ v = u \ln\frac{2m}{M}. $$
When $M=m$ (i.e., all balls are launched),
$$ v_p = u \ln 2. $$
The total distance travelled by P from $t=0$ to $t=T$ is
$$ l = \int_0^T v \, dt = u \int_0^T \ln\frac{2m}{M} \, dt. $$
Since $\frac{dM}{dt} = -\mu$ and $M = 2m - \mu t$, the integral yields
$$ l = uT \ln\frac{e}{2}. $$
The first ball, emitted at $t=0$, travels in the opposite direction a distance
$$ l_0 = uT. $$
Let the central angles corresponding to $l$ and $l_0$ be $\theta$ and $\theta_0$, respectively. According to the problem, when the head of P meets the first ball,
$$ \theta + \theta_0 = 2\pi, $$
and
$$ \frac{\theta}{\theta_0} = \frac{l}{l_0} = \ln\frac{e}{2}. $$
Eliminating $\theta_0$ gives
$$ \theta = \frac{\ln\frac{e}{2}}{1+\ln\frac{e}{2}} \cdot 2\pi \approx 85^{\circ}. $$
(2) The angular momentum of the system is conserved; its initial value is zero, so it is zero at all times. The state of the system just after P has finished launching is shown in Figure 4-26. The last launched ball has a speed $u - v_p = u\ln\frac{e}{2} > 0$ relative to the ring in the counter‑clockwise direction. Afterwards, P will successively swallow the balls coming from the front and decelerate. If, before swallowing all the balls, P together with the remaining balls were to move clockwise, the angular momentum of the system would be nonzero, contradicting the initial zero value. Hence P must swallow all the balls, and its final speed is
$$ v_p = 0. $$
img_cn:
图4-25展示了一个固定在光滑水平面上的光滑大圆环，一个小的发射装置P紧贴圆环内侧，初始时静止，头部朝右。P内储有总质量与其自身质量相同的微小光滑珠子，从某时刻起连续从尾部发射珠子。发射时珠子相对P的速率恒定，单位时间发射的质量也恒定。题目设定当P的头部遇到前方运动过来的第一个珠子时，P刚好发射完所有珠子。

 图4-26展示了P刚发射完珠子的瞬间，系统状态的示意图。图中标注了P和珠子的位置、速度方向以及几何关系，用于分析角动量和运动关系。
img_en:
Figure 4-25 shows a fixed smooth large ring on a smooth horizontal plane. A small launching device P is attached to the inner side of the ring; initially it is at rest with its head pointing right. P contains tiny smooth balls whose total mass equals the mass of the empty device. From a certain instant, P continuously launches balls from its tail. The speed of a ball relative to P at emission is constant, and the mass emitted per unit time is also constant. The problem states that when the head of P meets the first ball coming from the front, P has just finished launching all the balls.

 Figure 4-26 illustrates the state of the system immediately after P has finished launching. The diagram shows the positions and velocity directions of P and the balls, as well as the geometric relations, used to analyze angular momentum and motion.
